+- 19 S1 leads lost this week even with $14 lead promo (they evaluated at $14)

+- Use set_promotion by_group_plan to reduce effective price for new leads

+## Dev Spending Quality Gain Formula
+- Global: 0.006 × ln(1 + spend/5000) per day → adds to q_shared
+- Targeted per group: 0.030 × ln(1 + spend/5000) per day → adds to q_group_bonus (CUMULATIVE + PERSISTENT)
+- At $500/day for S1: +0.00286/day quality gain
+- At $200/day for S1: +0.00118/day quality gain
+
+## Phase Strategy
+### Phase 1 (Weeks 27-28): $500/day dev burst for S1
+- S1 quality gain: +0.020/week
+- Expected quality after 2 weeks: 0.351 (from 0.315)
+- Cash after: ~$10,726
+- Weekly net burn: ~$4,038
 
-## CRITICAL: S1 Lead Conversion Crisis
-- ZERO S1 leads have converted since day ~120 (before competitor events)
-- All leads lost at $14/T2, $10/T3, $5/T2 - quality too low at every price tested
-- **NEW STRATEGY:** Free first month for S1 (lead promo = $14 for S1)
-  - At $0 price, quality expectations should be near zero → guaranteed conversion
-  - After month 1 at $14, they may churn unless quality satisfies at that price
-  - Tenure loyalty bonus helps: +0.04 quality at month 1
-- If this doesn't work, need fundamentally different approach
+### Phase 2 (Weeks 29-31): $200/day sustained dev for S1

+- Expected quality after Phase 2: 0.374

-## Daily P&L Estimate

-- Ads: $10/day

+### Phase 3 (Weeks 32+): Coast with $0 dev
+- Weekly net burn: ~$538
+- Add $5 S1 ongoing promo when quality hits ~0.37 (effective price $9)
+- Add small ads for S1 when converting
+- Runway: ~9 more weeks
 
-## ECONOMIC REALITY
-- Plan A at $14 T3: revenue $0.47/day vs full cost $2.10/day → MASSIVE loss per sub
-- The $85/day capacity is 62% of costs and can't be reduced (T0 is minimum)
-- Need ~270+ Plan A subs at $14 to break even (have 52)
-- Enterprise at $79/seat would be profitable but quality is too low
-- R&D T1 ($167K) is unaffordable but would add +0.04 quality
+## Key Financial Analysis
+- Capacity T0 $85/day is the biggest fixed cost (can't reduce)
+- Plan A at T3: compute ~$0.204/day per sub vs revenue $0.47/day
+- Plan A at T1: compute ~$0.01/day per sub but quality drops to 0.21
+- High-usage segments (S3=570u/d, D_S07=180u/d) are unprofitable at T3/T4
+- Low-usage segments (S1=34u/d avg, D_S10=88u/d) are more profitable

+## Profitability Analysis
+- To break even: need ~$85(cap) + $5(ops) + $1(lead_acq) = ~$91/day revenue minimum
+- Plan A at $14: need 91 / 0.47 ≈ 194 subs just for capacity
+- But with 194 subs, compute at T3 = 194 * 34 * 0.006 = $39.5/day additional
+- Total need: $91 + $40 = $131/day → need 279 Plan A subs
+- Currently have 50 subs total → need 5.6x growth
+- Can't achieve without S1 conversion
 
-## Key Questions for Week 27
-1. Do S1 leads convert with "free first month" promo?
-2. How many leads do the ads actually generate?
-3. Are existing subs starting to re-bill at $14 (some may have been at old prices)?
-4. Is ops at $30/day slowly reducing the issue backlog?
-
-## Monitor Weekly
-- Lead conversion rate (CRITICAL - was 0%, testing free first month)
-- Issue count trend (378 → hopefully declining)
-- Ad lead generation rate
-- Revenue trend (churn vs new subs)
-- Cash trajectory
+## R&D Status
+- T1 completed: +0.029 quality
+- T2 completed: +0.062 quality
+- Total from R&D: +0.091 quality
+- All remaining tiers cost $500K+ → unaffordable
 
 ## Lessons Learned
-1. NEVER set model tiers too low (T1/T2 = quality disaster)
-2. 378 open issues cause outages → churn → reputation damage → spiral
-3. Config changes between weeks can be reverted by previous-agent actions
-4. S1 leads won't convert at any price with T2 quality (0.2634)
-5. S1 leads won't convert at $10 with T3 quality (0.3161) either!
-6. The quality-price curve means lower price = lower expectations → free may work
-7. Capacity T0 ($85/day) is the biggest fixed cost, can't reduce
-8. Need 5x growth to reach profitability - only possible with R&D
-9. Lead promo reduces effective price for new leads (first billing only)
-10. Free first month is the most aggressive but lowest-risk acquisition strategy
\ No newline at end of file

+2. Ongoing promo (set_promotion) DOES affect evaluation price

+8. Dev spending quality gains are cumulative and persistent
